domain_experience: staff engineer at a healthcare claims startup
knows where provider groups lose money on denials and prior auth
assets: deep domain understanding of denial and prior-auth failure modes
distribution: must rely on async channels or partners
credibility_signals: firsthand operational knowledge of reimbursement leakage
unfair_advantages: stronger problem selection than generic AI builders
can translate payer-policy and workflow friction into concrete tools

binding_constraints: No PHI or employer/customer-confidential data can be used
No consulting or sales calls removes the fastest revenue path
4 hours per week requires an async low-ops offer
Need to avoid adjacent-IP and moonlighting conflicts

opportunities.title: Async denial and prior-auth workflow kit
opportunities.thesis: Sell a narrow self-serve pack of templates, checklists, prompts, and synthetic examples for denial prevention and appeal preparation using only public rules and non-PHI inputs.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user knows the exact workflow breakdowns that cost provider groups money, so the assets can be concrete and operationally useful rather than generic AI templates.
opportunities.why_this_could_fail: Weak initial distribution and low defensibility if the assets are too broad or not updated.
validation_plan: Create one landing page with one sample asset and a $99-$299 price
Produce 3 sample assets using only public payer-policy sources and synthetic examples
Send 20 async messages to independent RCM operators or billing-shop owners
Post in 3 non-employer channels and track replies
Target 2 preorders or 1 paid pilot in 7 days
kill_criteria: Fewer than 5 meaningful replies after 20 async outreaches and 3 posts
Zero preorders and zero paid pilots after 7 days
Repeated requests for bespoke consulting instead of self-serve assets

opportunities.title: Public-policy denial intelligence subscription
opportunities.thesis: Turn public payer and CMS policy changes into operational alerts, denial-risk summaries, and workflow guidance for small provider organizations and outsourced billing teams.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user can interpret how policy changes translate into operational revenue leakage.
opportunities.why_this_could_fail: Needs consistent publishing and a credible distribution wedge; newsletter-only economics are weak.
validation_plan: Publish 3 sample updates for a single specialty
Offer an annual founding subscription to 20 async prospects
Target 3 paid founding subscribers before building software
kill_criteria: No paid founding subscribers after 20 targeted async offers
Content takes more than 4 hours per week to sustain

opportunities.title: Denial workflow copilot from kit to lightweight app
opportunities.thesis: Start with a paid workflow kit and evolve into a self-serve app that maps denial reasons and payer names to evidence checklists and policy-research steps without storing PHI.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user has strong edge in selecting the right workflow wedge and translating payer friction into guided prep steps.
opportunities.why_this_could_fail: Distribution remains hard, and the scope can easily drift into regulated advice or PHI handling if not tightly constrained.
validation_plan: Sell 5 copies of the workflow kit first
Interview buyers asynchronously via email or form on their top repetitive prep task
Build a tiny non-PHI form workflow assistant only after repeated requests
Target 3 repeat buyers or 5 paid kit sales as productization trigger
kill_criteria: No repeat demand after 5 kit sales
Buyers need direct handling, consulting, or PHI-heavy workflows
Product scope cannot stay inside public data plus non-PHI inputs

rejected_paths.title: Benchmark product built from employer-side denial data
rejected_paths.reason: Too much confidentiality, data-rights, and employer-conflict risk unless the data is independently sourced and clearly licensed.

rejected_paths.title: Broad consulting or managed denial-recovery service
rejected_paths.reason: Conflicts with the user's preference against consulting and sales calls, and exceeds the 4-hour-per-week constraint.

rejected_paths.title: Software-first healthcare AI platform
rejected_paths.reason: Too slow to validate with weak distribution, tight time budget, and regulated-scope risk.

next_actions: Review employment agreement for moonlighting, IP, and adjacency restrictions
Pick one specialty and one workflow wedge
Launch one paid landing page with explicit no-PHI and human-review disclaimers
Create 3 synthetic sample assets from public payer-policy materials
Run 20 async outreaches and 3 community posts in non-employer channels
Only build the lightweight app after 5 paid sales or 3 repeat-buyer signals
